business_ideas.idea: Economics framework - five value capture roles in any system
Vice businesses (adult content, gambling, CBD) - high margin, misunderstood

dynamics: Shaan drives economic framework discussion with precision; Sam engages with examples and pushes on specifics. Both passionate about demystifying how value splits across roles—intellectual debate with practical applications throughout.
frameworks: Five roles of value capture: logistics/labor (least capture, most work), operator/manager, capital, network/distribution, regulation/legal
Value inversely correlates with effort—manual labor captures least despite highest exertion
Position choice determines life outcome more than effort level
learnings: Construction/house building illustrates inequality—laborers work hardest but capture least value
Each economic system has exact five roles; only choice is which role to occupy
Vice businesses (adult, gambling, cannabis) generate outsized revenue due to stigma creating supply gaps
Regulatory capture and license control create moat for businesses in restricted categories
Fan fiction and niche communities represent underexploited value pools

banger_quotes.quote: There are five ways to make money in any system—pick which role you want
Vice businesses work because good people avoid them, leaving money on table

notable: Deep structural framework episode—Shaan's five-role model becomes recurring MFM teaching tool, explaining why certain positions yield wealth while others don't despite equivalent effort
